results.title: Support for Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment
results.description: Organizations face a significant security risk when users inadvertently overshare or mishandle sensitive internal files. The integration of AI tools, such as Microsoft Copilot, intensifies this risk, as these tools can access information within the improperly shared files, leading to the potential exposure of sensitive data to unauthorized parties. Therefore, implementing proactive monitoring and control measures is crucial to safeguard organizational security and prevent accidental data leaks. As part of this feature, the following enhancements are available in the Zscaler Admin Console: SaaS Workflows: Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment SaaS Workflows is introduced to streamline data protection and compliance, which is designed to automate processes and enable bulk remediation actions. A significant current capability within these workflows is the new Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment page. This is crucial in helping organizations mitigate risks and guarantee a secure and prepared onboarding experience for Microsoft applications. You can access the dashboard from Analytics > Internet & SaaS > Analytics > SaaS Workflows > Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment. See image. To learn more, see About SaaS Workflows , About Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment , and Viewing Drill Down of All Assets . SaaS Security Insights Logs and SaaS Security Assets Logs Filters and columns, Accessibility Flags and Label, are added to the SaaS Security Insights Logs page and SaaS Security Assets with Incidents page (Analytics > Internet & SaaS > Analytics > SaaS Security > Assets) for collaboration applications, email applications, and file sharing applications. See image. To learn more, see About SaaS Security Insights Logs and Understanding the Assets Report: Assets with Incidents . NSS Feeds You can add the following fields for the Microsoft Copilot Readiness Assessment when configuring SaaS Security NSS or Cloud NSS Feed Output Format. These fields apply to collaboration, email, and file applications: %s{labelname} %s{accessibility_flags} %s{copilot_accessible} To learn more, see NSS Feed Output Format: SaaS Security Logs .

results.title: Cloud Custom IPS Enhancements
results.description: Custom IPS is supported on Zscaler's public cloud, allowing you to create and deploy custom IPS signature rules without requiring any additional infrastructure (previously required a Private Service Edge deployment). You can inspect traffic transiting the Zscaler cloud against your custom signatures, irrespective of whether it is forwarded to a Public, Private, or Virtual Service Edge. To enable Custom IPS with your Advanced Firewall subscription, contact your Zscaler Account team. With this upgrade, the following enhancements are also made to Custom IPS: Applicable to decrypted web traffic (decrypted via SSL Inspection) in addition to non-web traffic. Extended support for HTTP protocol-aware signatures, so signature rules can include HTTP-specific options. Signature rule validation occurs in two stages before deployment: static validation (currently supported) that checks rules for syntax errors, duplication, and based on other predefined conditions; dynamic validation (newly added) that runs signatures against designated packet captures to ensure efficient resource usage and optimal performance. The signature is enabled after both validations are completed. These transitions are indicated by new statuses. View the newly added actions to initiate dynamic validation of signature rules. Logs showing custom signature matches for better visibility and easier troubleshooting. Increased the custom signature rule limit to 1,000 (previously 500). To learn more, see About Custom IPS Signature Rules , Configuring Custom IPS Signature Rules , and Specifications & Limitations for Custom IPS Signature Rules . In the Add Signature Rule (and Edit Signature Rule) window, a Save & Promote button is added to trigger dynamic validation of the rule. Save image. The Enable Signature Rule toggle button is not accessible until the rule moves to the Enable Ready state with the successful completion of dynamic validation. There is no change to the existing Save option, which continues to save the rule after successful static validation. On the Custom Signature Rules page, a Promote All button is added to initiate dynamic validation of all rules at once. See image.

results.title: Support for Workday Tenant RaaS-based API Access
results.description: Workday tenants now support RaaS-based API access which enables secure, programmatic retrieval of data and management through web services. Existing Workday tenants need to be reauthenticated by editing the tenant and revalidating. To learn more, see Adding SaaS Application Tenants and About SaaS Application Tenants .

results.title: Support in SaaS Security Data at Rest DLP Policy for Quarantine of Sensitive Content in Microsoft Teams
results.description: The SaaS Security Data at Rest Scanning policy supports a new option to quarantine sensitive content in Microsoft Teams. You can specify a tombstone message that end users see when messages or files in Microsoft Teams are quarantined. See image. After a message or file has been quarantined in Microsoft Teams, admins can use the SaaS Assets Report to review and remove the quarantined content. See image. To learn more, see Configuring the SaaS Security API DLP Policy , About Quarantine Tombstone File Templates , and SaaS Assets Report: Assets with Incidents .

results.title: Enhancement to Gen AI Prompt Configuration
results.description: The generative AI prompt configuration is extended to the Grammarly application . As part of this change, the Grammarly option is added to the Policy > URL & Cloud App Control > Advanced Policy Settings page. See image. To learn more, see Configuring Advanced Policy Settings . Added Grammarly Capability to URL & Cloud App Control Policy Settings Endpoint A new Boolean field, enableGrammarlyPrompt , is available for the AdvancedUrlFilteringCloudAppOptions model in the /advancedUrlFilterAndCloudAppSettings APIs. This field allows users to use generative AI prompts with Grammarly. To learn more, go to AdvancedUrlFilteringCloudAppOptions from URL & Cloud App Control Policy Settings .

results.title: Outbound Email DLP Quarantine with Release or Delete Emails in Workflow Automation
results.description: Zscaler now supports the Quarantine action when configuring Outbound Email DLP policies for Microsoft Exchange. When Quarantine is selected, emails that match policy criteria are quarantined directly in Microsoft Exchange. To use the Quarantine feature, go to Policy > Email DLP and then select Quarantine under Action in the applicable policy rule. See image. Email Data Loss Prevention (DLP) incidents that Microsoft has quarantined are displayed in Workflow Automation. In Workflow Automation, you can release or delete the email to one or more of its recipients using one of the following methods: On the Incident Details page, an admin can select the Release Email Quarantine action from the Actions drop-down menu and select the recipients to release the email to or delete the email. See image. When an end user responds to a user notification or escalation notification that is associated with a quarantined email incident, the notifications are enhanced to include a new Release Email to Recipients section that includes release quarantine options. To enable this feature for Workflow Automation, contact Zscaler Support. To learn more, see: About Outbound Email Policy Configuring Outbound Email Policy Rules Step-by-Step Configuration Guide for Zscaler Outbound Email DLP Configuring Microsoft Exchange for Zscaler Outbound Email DLP Adding Email Tenants Managing Incidents Viewing & Managing Incident Details

results.title: Advanced SSPM Support for Lucidchart
results.description: Advanced SSPM supports a new platform, Lucidchart. You can onboard a Lucidchart tenant from the 3rd-Party App Governance Admin Portal. See image. To learn more, see Connecting Your Platforms to Advanced SSPM .

results.title: Support for Zendesk as an API-Based Platform in Advanced SSPM
results.description: Advanced SSPM supports Zendesk as an API-based platform. You can onboard a Zendesk tenant from the 3rd-Party App Governance Admin Portal. See image. To learn more, see Connecting Your Platforms to Advanced SSPM .

results.title: Advanced SaaS Security Posture Management Support for Oracle Financials Cloud
results.description: You can configure Advanced SaaS Security Posture Management (SSPM) for Oracle Financials Cloud tenants. Select the SSPM Scan checkbox when onboarding a tenant to enable the Advanced SSPM scan capability for the specific tenant. See image. The SSPM Scan checkbox is enabled only when you have Advanced SSPM enabled for your tenant. To learn more, see What Is Advanced Posture Management? and Adding SaaS Application Tenants .

results.title: Document Classification and Logging for SaaS Security API, Email, and Endpoint DLP
results.description: AI or machine language classification is extended to support around 200 new document types across 10 common document categories. As part of this extended support, Insights Logs and the Nanolog Streaming Service (NSS) are enhanced to provide enriched auto-classification of documents that are uploaded or downloaded during transactions. The following enhancements are available in the ZIA Admin Portal: Updates to Insights Logs A new filter and column, Subdocument Type, is added to the Endpoint DLP Insights Logs and SaaS Security Insights Logs pages for email applications (e.g., Gmail) to capture, categorize, and log document subtypes related to administrative documents, invoices, expense reports, and more. See image. To learn more, see Endpoint DLP Insights Logs: Filters , Endpoint DLP Insights Logs: Columns , and About SaaS Security Insights Logs . Updates to NSS Feeds The field %s{doc_subtype} can be added to the Feed Output Format when configuring an NSS or Cloud NSS feed for Endpoint DLP logs. Additionally, the field %s{upload_doc_subtype} is available for SaaS Security feeds configured for email applications (e.g., Gmail). Both fields log the subtype (e.g., Financial Statements: Income Statements (Profit and Loss Statements)) of uploaded or downloaded documents. To learn more, see NSS Feed Output Format: Endpoint DLP Logs ​​​​​​and NSS Feed Output Format: SaaS Security Logs .

results.title: Expanded SSPM Controls for Microsoft Copilot
results.description: Advanced SaaS Security Posture Management (SSPM) expands its coverage for Microsoft Copilot with 15 new SSPM controls, providing deeper visibility and stronger security posture assessment.

results.title: New Bandwidth Control Fields for Transactions
results.description: Web Insights are enhanced to allow users to view the latency caused by bandwidth control on transactions in the ZIA Admin Portal. Web Insights Logs The following new columns and filters are available in the Web Insights Logs: Request Latency Due to Bandwidth Control (ms) Response Latency Due to Bandwidth Control (ms) Total Latency Due to Bandwidth Control (ms) See image. To learn more, see Web Insights Logs: Columns and Web Insights Logs: Filters . NSS Feeds The fields %d{txn_delay_req} and %d{txn_delay_resp} can be added to the Feed Output Format when configuring an NSS or Cloud NSS feed for web logs. These fields display the bandwidth transaction request delay and the bandwidth transaction response delay in milliseconds, respectively. To learn more, see NSS Feed Output Format: Web Logs .

results.title: Application Investigation in Endpoint Data Scan
results.description: Zscaler's Application Investigation is a solution that provides comprehensive visibility and automated risk management for an organization's application ecosystem by delivering a clear view of all software in use, including unauthorized AI tools and unapproved browsers. By automatically identifying vulnerable or suspicious applications, Application Investigation enables administrators to prioritize security efforts and focus on the highest-threat categories and vulnerabilities across the organization. The Application Investigation page (Analytics > Endpoint Data Scan > Application Investigation) provides insights into executable files and applications stored on endpoints, gathering data on installed software, running processes, and libraries. This is crucial not only for compliance, but also for monitoring, auditing, and identifying vulnerabilities or unauthorized software. As part of this change, the Inventory > Applications tab is now moved to Application Investigation > Inventory tab. See image. To learn more, see About the Application Investigation Overview , About the Application Investigation Inventory , Analyzing Applications Details , and Accessing and Navigating the Endpoint Data Scan Report .

results.title: DLP Operational in Endpoint Data Scan
results.description: The Zscaler DLP Operational page (Analytics > Endpoint Data Scan > DLP Operational) provides insights into the endpoint's status, thereby ensuring that the system functions properly and as designed to avoid sensitive data leakage. See image. To learn more, see About DLP Operational Dashboard .

results.title: Enhancement to Firewall Policies Endpoints
results.description: A new query parameter, ruleLabelId , is available for the GET /firewallFilteringRules and GET /firewallFilteringRules/count endpoints. The ruleLabelId query parameter allows you to filter Firewall Filtering rules based on the rule label ID. To learn more, go to GET /firewallFilteringRules and GET /firewallFilteringRules/count from Firewall Policies . To learn more about each endpoint, see the API Reference and API Rate Limit Summary .

results.title: Enhancements to Admin Role Management
results.description: On the Internet & SaaS > Role Management page, in the Policy & Components > Access Control tab, the URL Filtering and Cloud App Control policy permissions are separated to provide admins more granular control over those features. See image. To learn more, see Adding Admin Roles .

results.title: Exact Data Matching in Endpoint Data Scan
results.description: Zscaler has introduced Exact Data Matching (EDM) for Endpoint Data Loss Prevention (DLP) which provides protection for structured sensitive data. This feature utilizes a unique hybrid architecture where the endpoint performs local prefiltering to identify candidate files for cloud inspection, ensuring maximum accuracy without compromising device performance or bandwidth. You can access Exact Data Matching (EDM) page from Analytics > Endpoint Data Scan > Sensitive Information. To enable this feature, contact your Zscaler Account team. See image. To learn more, see Understanding Exact Data Matching (EDM) and About Endpoint Data Scan Sensitive Information: Exact Data Matching .

results.title: Expanded SSPM Controls for Salesforce
results.description: Advanced SaaS Security Posture Management (SSPM) expands its coverage for Salesforce with three new SSPM controls, providing deeper visibility and stronger security posture assessment.

results.title: Filtering by User Group in Endpoint Data Scan
results.description: Administrators can filter data on the Endpoint Data Scan page (Analytics > Endpoint Data Scan > Endpoint Data Scan) by a specific user group. See image. To learn more, see About Endpoint Data Scan .

results.title: Share Files Externally in Endpoint Data Scan
results.description: Share Files Externally provides a capability for Endpoint DLP that allows authorized users to securely share sensitive files with external partners by encrypting the data into a secure HTML format, ensuring that only verified recipients can access the content. You can access the Share Files Externally page from Analytics > Endpoint Data Scan > Sensitive Information. See image. To learn more, see About Endpoint Data Scan Sensitive Information: Share Files Externally , Configuring Share File Externally Settings , and Understanding Share Files Externally .

results.title: SSL Inspection and SSL Policy Renamed to SSL/TLS Inspection and SSL/TLS Policy
results.description: The labels SSL Inspection and SSL policy are renamed to SSL/TLS Inspection and SSL/TLS policy. See image. Refer to the following table for a list of updated labels: Old UI Labels New UI Labels SSL Inspection (Policy > SSL Inspection) SSL/TLS Inspection (Policy > SSL/TLS Inspection) SSL Inspection Policy (Policy > SSL Inspection > SSL Inspection Policy) SSL/TLS Inspection Policy (Policy > SSL/TLS Inspection > SSL/TLS Inspection Policy) Configure SSL Inspection Policy (Policy > SSL Inspection > Configure SSL Inspection Policy) Configure SSL/TLS Inspection Policy (Policy > SSL/TLS Inspection > Configure SSL/TLS Inspection Policy) Add SSL Inspection Rule (Policy > SSL Inspection > Add SSL Inspection Rule) Add SSL/TLS Inspection Rule (Policy > SSL/TLS Inspection > Add SSL/TLS Inspection Rule) Edit SSL Inspection Rule (Policy > SSL Inspection > Edit SSL Inspection Rule) Edit SSL/TLS Inspection Rule (Policy > SSL/TLS Inspection > Edit SSL/TLS Inspection Rule)

results.title: Support for Certificate-based Authentication with Microsoft Applications for a Custom Zscaler Connector
results.description: When you create a custom Zscaler connector for a Microsoft application, you have the option to choose whether you want to provide a client secret or a private key in the Zscaler Admin Console so that the Zscaler service can access the application. SharePoint, Copilot, and Microsoft 365 require a private key for onboarding, but all other supported Microsoft applications support both options. See image. To learn more, see Adding SaaS Application Tenants and Authorizing a Custom Zscaler Connector for Microsoft Applications .

results.title: Support for CIDR and Regex on Network Share DLP Resources
results.description: The Zscaler service supports using Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) ranges and regular expressions for advanced matching when creating or editing network shares for Endpoint Data Loss Prevention (DLP) resources. The following image shows a regular expression that matches all network servers that start with "srv" and the match is case insensitive: See Image. To learn more, see Adding DLP Resources and Editing DLP Resources .

results.title: Support for Network Type on Endpoint DLP Policy Rules
results.description: The Zscaler service supports specifying a network type (e.g., Trusted, Off-Trusted, VPN) for the Network Share, Printing, and Device Control channels when creating policy rules for Endpoint Data Loss Prevention (DLP). Endpoints must be running Zscaler Client Connector version 4.8.600 or later (Windows) or version 4.7.1 or later (macOS) for policy rules to enforce the network type. To enable this feature, contact your Zscaler Account team. See Image. To learn more, see Configuring Endpoint DLP Policy Rules and About Trusted Networks .

results.title: Updates to Recipient Email Profile Endpoints
results.description: You can create, update, and delete recipient email profiles, retrieve a list of all recipient email profiles, and obtain the count of recipient email profiles for an organization using the following endpoints: GET /emailRecipientProfile POST /emailRecipientProfile GET /emailRecipientProfile/{profileId} PUT /emailRecipientProfile/{profileId} DELETE /emailRecipientProfile/{profileId} GET /emailRecipientProfile/count To learn more about each endpoint, see the API Reference and API Rate Limit Summary .
